2026-04-09T08:56:52.6926037Z with:

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-09T08:56:53.9480967Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T08:56:37.567958957Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #57773) by @ArcturusZhang\n\n## What Changed\n\nThe `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` library (v1.0.0-beta.1) was introduced as a new provisioning package for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, generated using the new provisioning generator. It includes resource types such as `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, and `AdvancedThreatProtection`.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Examples section is completely empty** — no code snippets demonstrating how to create a MySQL Flexible Server or related resources\n- **No sample test file exists** — `tests/Samples/` directory has no `.cs` files; there are no snippet-backed code examples\n- **\"Authenticate the Client\" section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication works with provisioning libraries\n- **\"Key concepts\" section contains only one generic sentence** — no description of the specific resource types available or how they relate to each other\n- **\"Next steps\" section is empty** — no links to related resources or documentation\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` (edit)\n\nReplace the empty `## Examples` section with a code snippet placeholder, and fill in the empty sections. Follow the pattern from `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/README.md` as a reference.\n\nReplace the current README content with:\n\n```markdown\n# Azure Provisioning MySql client library for .NET\n\nAzure.Provisioning.MySql simplifies declarative resource provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers in .NET.\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the client library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.MySql --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n\u003e You must have an [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/).\n\n### Authenticate the Client\n\nThis library provisions Azure infrastructure using the Azure Developer CLI (azd) or Azure Bicep. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling — no explicit client authentication code is required in the provisioning definition itself.\n\n## Key concepts\n\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\n\nThe main resource types provided by this library are:\n\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — Represents an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instance\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — Represents a database within a flexible server\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — Manages firewall rules to control access to the server\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — Manages server configuration parameters\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — Configures Azure Active Directory administrators for the server\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** / **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`** — Manages server backups\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Manages scheduled maintenance windows\n- **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Configures Advanced Threat Protection for the server\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`** — Manages private endpoint connections\n\n## Examples\n\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\n\nThis example demonstrates how to create a MySQL Flexible Server with administrator login credentials.\n\n```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n- File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).\n- Check [previous questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/azure+.net) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags.\n\n## Next steps\n\n- Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/mysql/flexible-server/overview)\n- Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the base provisioning library\n- See the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/) for deployment tooling\n\n## Contributing\n\nFor details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing guide][cg].\n\nThis project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit \u003chttps://cla.microsoft.com\u003e.\n\nWhen you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the bot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories using our CLA.\n\nThis project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact \u003copencode@microsoft.com\u003e with any other questions or comments.\n\n\u003c!-- LINKS --\u003e\n[cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md\n[coc]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/\n[coc_faq]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` with a test demonstrating how to create a basic MySQL Flexible Server. Use `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/tests/Samples/` as a reference if it exists, otherwise model after `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`.\n\nThe test file should:\n- Be in namespace `Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests`\n- Use `[Test]` attribute (NUnit)\n- Wrap the snippet content with `#region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` / `#endregion`\n- Demonstrate creating an `Infrastructure`, adding parameters (admin login, admin password), creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` with `MySqlFlexibleServerSku`, `MySqlFlexibleServerVersion`, `MySqlFlexibleServerStorage`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties`, and `MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability`, then adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\n\nExample structure for `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing Azure.Provisioning.Primitives;\nusing Azure.Provisioning.MySql;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests;\n\npublic class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer\n{\n    [Test]\n    [Category(\"Samples\")]\n    public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer()\n    {\n        #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic\n        Infrastructure infra = new();\n\n        ProvisioningParameter adminLogin =\n            new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string))\n            {\n                Description = \"The administrator username of the server.\"\n            };\n        infra.Add(adminLogin);\n\n        ProvisioningParameter adminPass =\n            new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string))\n            {\n                Description = \"The administrator password of the server.\",\n                IsSecure = true\n            };\n        infra.Add(adminPass);\n\n        MySqlFlexibleServer server =\n            new(nameof(server))\n            {\n                Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\n                {\n                    Name = \"Standard_D2ds_v4\",\n                    Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose\n                },\n                CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default,\n                Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021,\n                AdministratorLogin = adminLogin,\n                AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass,\n                Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage\n                {\n                    StorageSizeInGB = 20\n                },\n                Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\n                {\n                    BackupRetentionDays = 7,\n                    GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\n                },\n                HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability\n                {\n                    Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled\n                }\n            };\n        infra.Add(server);\n\n        MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database =\n            new(nameof(database))\n            {\n                Parent = server,\n                Name = \"mydb\"\n            };\n        infra.Add(database);\n        #endregion\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**README snippet placeholder location:** In `README.md` under `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`, after the description line, insert:\n```\n```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic\n```\n```\n(a closing ` ``` ` follows — the `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the content)\n\n**Snippet name:** `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic`\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` is currently missing or has empty content for the following sections:\n\n| Section | Status |\n|---|---|\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present |\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty — add authentication guidance |\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — add resource type descriptions |\n| Examples | ❌ Empty — add code snippet |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty — add links to related resources |\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from the new test file and injects the code into `README.md` placeholder blocks. Check `git diff` after running to confirm README was updated correctly\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — only needed if any public types or members were added or changed\n5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` — ensures consistent code formatting\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[MySql] Docs: Add examples and key concepts to Azure.Provisioning.MySql README"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
